question: A team wants to attach `trace-id:3004` as an immutable label that travels with every job created during a process instance, for log correlation. Which job property is designed for this?

options.text: Tags — immutable labels copied from the process instance to each job at creation time.
Custom headers — static metadata defined per task in the BPMN model and shared across all instances.
Variables — the contextual business data of the process instance required by the worker.
Key — the unique identifier used to complete or fail the job.

a.text: The job-workers concept page lists Tags as immutable labels copied from the process instance at job creation, with `reference:1234`, `team:accounting`, and `trace-id:3004` as the docs' own examples — exactly the per-instance correlation use case.

b.text: Custom headers are static metadata defined on the BPMN task in the model (e.g. a Slack channel a reusable worker reads), not copied per-instance from the process at job creation time.

c.text: Variables hold mutable contextual business data the worker uses to do its work; they are not designed as immutable correlation labels.

d.text: Key is the unique identifier the system assigns to a job for use with `complete`/`fail` commands — a system identifier, not a free-form label.

explanation: Tags are immutable labels copied from the process instance into every job at the moment the job is created; the docs name `reference`, `team`, and `trace-id` as canonical examples. Custom headers are per-task static metadata in the model, Variables are mutable business payload, and Key is the system handle used by `complete`/`fail` commands — none of those match a per-instance, immutable correlation label.
